# Mahbubul Alam

> Documentary photographer based in Dhaka, Bangladesh, documenting human–wildlife conflict, nature, and the environment.

Mahbubul Alam is a documentary photographer whose work focuses on communities living alongside wild elephants in Bangladesh's forests and farmlands. His photographs have been published in the Dhaka Tribune and exhibited at institutions including the United Nations in Bangladesh and the Centre for Policy Dialogue (CPD). He has received national photography awards and been shortlisted for UNESCO's Youth Eyes on the Silk Roads international photo contest.

## Series

- [The Cost of Coexistence](https://mahbubulalamnayeem.me/series/cost-of-coexistence.html): Ongoing documentary series (2023–) on human-elephant conflict in the Chittagong Hill Tracts and Sylhet regions of Bangladesh.

## Sections

- [Biography](https://mahbubulalamnayeem.me/#biography): Background and profile of the photographer
- [Press](https://mahbubulalamnayeem.me/#press): Press coverage including Dhaka Tribune Spotlight, May 2024
- [Exhibitions](https://mahbubulalamnayeem.me/#exhibitions): Exhibition history including UN80 Youth Photo Exhibition and Asian Photo Fest
- [Awards](https://mahbubulalamnayeem.me/#awards): Awards including winner of the 13th Photography Carnival "Muhurto" 2026 and UNESCO shortlist

## Social

- [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/_through_my__eyes): Photography portfolio on Instagram
- [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/mahbubulalamnayeem): Facebook profile

## Optional

- [The Cost of Coexistence — Full Gallery](https://mahbubulalamnayeem.me/series/cost-of-coexistence.html): 26-photo documentary gallery on human-elephant conflict in Bangladesh
